page.title: Customers
page.subtitle: Customer health, segments, suggested actions
page.empty: No customers with linked data yet
page.errorBody: Could not load data. Please try again in a moment.

activeCustomers.title: Active customers
activeCustomers.def: Customers with at least one purchase in the last 60 days at period end.
activeCustomers.read: Higher = wider active base. Threshold configurable in Settings.

newCustomers.title: New customers
newCustomers.def: Customers acquired in the period (their very first purchase falls here).
newCustomers.calc: Distinct customer_id whose MIN(date) is within [start, end].

purchasingCustomers.title: Purchasing customers
purchasingCustomers.def: Customers with at least one sale in the selected period.
purchasingCustomers.calc: Distinct customer_id present in sales_records during the period.
purchasingCustomers.read: Higher = base that generated cash in the period.

totalRevenue.title: Period revenue
totalRevenue.def: Sum of sales recorded in the selected period.
totalRevenue.calc: Σ amount over sales_records where date ∈ [start, end].
totalRevenue.read: Higher = more cash generated. Compare to previous period.

avgCustomerValue.title: Average customer value
avgCustomerValue.def: Average revenue per customer in the period.
avgCustomerValue.read: Higher = customers spending more. Grows with upsell.

top10Share.title: Top-10 concentration
top10Share.def: Share of revenue taken by the 10 largest customers in the period.
top10Share.read: Above 60 % = high risk: few customers carry the revenue.

atRiskCount.title: At-risk customers
atRiskCount.def: Customers with churn risk score ≥ 60 (out of 100).
atRiskCount.read: Contact them to prevent loss. See card below for breakdown.

inactiveRate.title: Inactive rate
inactiveRate.def: Share of customers without purchases for 180+ days.
inactiveRate.read: Below 30 % = healthy base. Above 50 % = re-engagement needed.

infobox.def: How dependent your revenue is on the largest customers.
infobox.calc: Share of top-N customers over total revenue.
infobox.read: Top 5 above 50 % = highly dependent. Below 30 % = well distributed.

infobox.def: How many customers fall into each segment and their revenue weight.
infobox.calc: Count + revenue sum grouped by segment.
infobox.read: Click a slice to filter the table below.

table.title: Customer list
table.exportCsvGdprHint: The CSV includes an unsubscribe_url column with a one-click revocation link per customer. Use it as a merge tag in your marketing campaign footers (e.g. {{unsubscribe_url}} on Mailchimp / Brevo) to comply with GDPR Art. 7(3).

actions.comingSoonHint: Email / WhatsApp / reminder buttons will activate in the next release.

cohort.title: Cohort retention table
cohort.subtitle: How many of the customers acquired in each month are still active in subsequent months.

infobox.def: Shows how many customers acquired in a month are still active in subsequent months.
infobox.calc: Per cohort: # active customers at month N / cohort size × 100.
infobox.read: Flat curve = stable retention. Steep = rapid loss.

dialog.mailtoTooltip: Opens the email program set as your system default (Mail / Outlook / Thunderbird). Only works if one is configured.
dialog.hint: Gmail web and Outlook web open the compose window in your browser (always works). "Default mail" only works if you have a system-default email program.

essential.recontactHint: Inactive with marketing consent: click to see the list
essential.advanced: Advanced analysis (detailed KPIs, concentration, revenue by segment)

crossSell.title: {{count}} customers joined a retreat but never booked a consultation
crossSell.hint: The most natural cross-sell: they already trust you. The message is ready.
